member.name: Julia Buckley
member.synopsis: Julia Buckley is the Labour MP for Shrewsbury , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Facilities Grants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of reviewing the financial limit for the disabled facilities grant.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In England, we continue to fund the Disabled Facilities Grant (DFG) which is delivered by local authorities. This grant helps eligible older and disabled people on low incomes to adapt their homes to make them safe and suitable for their needs.The upper l...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Urban Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: ?To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of broadband provision in historic town centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We want everyone in the UK to have access to secure, resilient and high-quality digital infrastructure and are committed to 99% gigabit coverage by 2032. 88% of UK premises already have gigabit broadband access, improving to 92% in urban areas. Rollout is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Rabies: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to secure access to supplies of the rabies vaccination for those travelling to countries where rabies is present; and when he expects supplies of the rabies vacc
writtenQuestions.answerText: Pre-exposure rabies vaccines for travellers are made available to the public through private travel health services, including general practices, pharmacies, and specialist travel clinics. The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) does not centrally procure r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the Infected Blood Compensation Scheme's timeline is to contact people eligible for payments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The delivery of compensation is a matter for the Infected Blood Compensation Authority (IBCA) The IBCA Framework Document, published in March 2025, sets out the timelines agreed between IBCA and Cabinet Office; namely for the bulk of infected people to be...

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Medical Records
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether there are plans to allow the electronic transfer of GP records when people move from Wales to England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government's 10-Year Health Plan includes the objective of delivering a Single Patient Record. This will allow health and care providers, wherever they deliver care in England, to access the key information they need from an individual’s medical recor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Cross Border Cooperation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking under GBR to increase cross border rail connections, such as the Wrexham-Shropshire-Midlands Railway.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation

writtenQuestions.heading: Office of Rail and Road
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how competition regulations on state aid are applied by the Office of Rail and Road when assessing applications for Open Access rail services; and what changes are expected following the ORR's move into her Dep
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the current framework, the Office of Rail and Road (ORR) is responsible for making access decisions in line with its statutory duties. Moving forward, this will change as Great British Railways takes over responsibility for managing access to the ne...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Greater London
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, which English counties do not have a direct train link to London; and whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of the lack of such links on those counties.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The only English county that does not have a direct train service from London is Shropshire.The department requires its operators to plan services and rail timetables that are designed to meet expected passenger demand and provide value for money for the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, of the £21 billion of new local transport funding announced across the Comprehensive Spending Review period, how much funding is allocated to (a) Established Mayoral Strategic Authorities, (b) Mayoral Strategic
writtenQuestions.answerText: Established Mayoral Strategic Authorities will receive c£7.7bn of transport funding through their integrated settlement over the Spending Review period from 2026/27 to 2029/30 (to 2028/29 for Resource Funding). Mayoral Strategic Authorities with a mayor i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood and Water Management Act 2010
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department intends to bring section 42 of the Flood and Water Management Act 2010 into force.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to addressing the longstanding issues with private sewerage. Government recognises that current arrangements of sewer adoption need review. We have recently consulted on the adoption of shared amenities, including SuDS and sewe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department is taking steps to develop a single national fibre broadband coverage map that identifies gaps in connectivity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of fast and reliable broadband connectivity across the UK and is committed to identifying and addressing gaps in coverage, in line with our ambition to achieve 99% gigabit broadband coverage by 2032.Ofcom is respon...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Intestacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of reforming Bona Vacantia arrangements to permit local authorities to take ownership of unclaimed residential properties for public use.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The revenue from the collection of bona vacantia, including that from the open market sales of any residential property, is transferred annually to the Consolidated Fund for the general funding of HM Government expenditure for the general benefit of all c...

writtenQuestions.heading: Intestacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the arrangements for managing unclaimed estates and properties under Bona Vacantia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The collection and disposal of bona vacantia monies arising from the estates of deceased people & dissolved companies is managed by the Treasury Solicitor as the Crown’s Nominee, with the proceeds passing each year to HM Treasury. Such arrangements ar...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, when veterans with more complex circumstances can expect to receive their Remediable Service Statements under the Armed Forces Pension Scheme 2015 remedy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While there is currently no specific date for the completion of these cases, the Ministry of Defence (MOD) is working through them as quickly as possible and is monitoring progress with the contractor on a weekly basis.The outstanding cases are inherently...

writtenQuestions.heading: Church of England
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Battersea, representing the Church Commissioners, when the Church will be opening the Church of England Redress Scheme to applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the Abuse Redress Measure receiving Royal Assent on the 18th December 2025, the National Church Institutions (NCIs) are finalising the technical operational details before the scheme launches. The NCIs are working with a multi-stakeholder Steeri...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Mayors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if she will take steps to ensure that Metro Mayors retain roles in heavy rail governance under the provisions of the Railways Bill.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Great British Railways (GBR) will work in partnership with Mayoral Strategic Authorities, underpinned by statutory roles outlined in the Railways Bill. The Railways Bill enables cooperation between GBR and Mayoral Strategic Authorities, allowing for infor...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways Bill
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how the Railways Bill will ensure that access rights to the network are fair, transparent and enforceable, particularly where Great British Rail will both manage infrastructure and operate services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new access framework within the Railways Bill will ensure that GBR will determine the best use of the network capacity for all operators in accordance with its statutory duties. New legislation will include key safeguards for third party operators, en...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Fares
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she will take to assess passenger affordability when setting rail fares.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Passenger affordability is a top priority for this Government when setting rail fares. That is why this year we have taken the historic step of freezing regulated rail fares for the first time in 30 years, putting money back in hard working people’s pocke...

writtenQuestions.heading: Great British Railways
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether the Railways Bill will set out the duties of the Great British Railways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Railways Bill includes a range of duties which will apply across the activities of GBR. Clause 18 sets the general duties which will apply to GBR, the ORR, the Secretary of State for Transport, and Scottish and Welsh Ministers. The general duties incl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways Bill
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how the Railways Bill will support improved connectivity for communities currently without such links.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Railways Bill will streamline the current fragmented system by establishing Great British Railways (GBR) as a new ‘directing mind’ for the industry, unifying track and train under one public body to deliver better services for passengers and customers...

items.text: Type of land/property: Residential property (1 bed Flat) Number of properties: 1 Location: Hamburg, Germany Ownership details: Co-owned with 2 step-family members (Registered 3 August 2024)
Non Executive Director of Shropshire Towns & Rural (STAR) housing association. This is an unpaid role. Date interest ended: 1 August 2024 (Registered 18 July 2024; updated 16 July 2025)
Unpaid Unitary Councillor on Shropshire Council. Date interest ended: 1 May 2025 (Registered 18 July 2024; updated 9 July 2025)
Unpaid role as elected councillor on Bridgnorth Town council. Date interest ended: 1 January 2025 (Registered 3 August 2024; updated 16 July 2025)

I was elected as an officer of the Labour Rural Research Group (LRRG), a formally constituted group within the Labour Party focused on rural policy development and advocacy. This is an unpaid role. I contribute to the strategic direction of policy discussions. Date interest arose: 12 January 2026 (Registered 14 April 2026)
